untranslatable_reason: This signal requires detection of behavioral patterns (non-speculative motivation, mechanical rebalancing, time-weighted execution, counterparty intent) and relative information advantage vs. market consensus. These are market microstructure concepts that cannot be operationalized using only OHLCV price/volume technical indicators. The signal requires: (1) order flow analysis and counterparty classification, (2) behavioral pattern recognition beyond price action, (3) relative information advantage measurement. None of these can be derived from the allowed indicator set (moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, basic volume ratios).
translation_notes: Signal ID MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_1_1 is fundamentally about exploiting information asymmetry through counterparty identification and behavioral inference. While the rule is conceptually sound for trading (zero-sum game, profit from informed vs. uninformed traders), it requires market microstructure data (order book patterns, trade classification algorithms, behavioral fingerprinting) not available in a standard technical indicator backtest engine. A practical implementation would require: order flow imbalance indicators, volume-weighted price analysis, or machine learning classification of order motivation—none of which can be expressed with the provided indicator constraints.
